issues.description: Class-based assertions used to verify badge variant (destructive/primary/muted). Tests should assert on ARIA attributes or data attributes for state, not CSS class names.
issues.rule: E2E Selectors - Never use Tailwind classes to select or assert state
issues.fix: Use data-variant or aria attributes on the Badge component and assert on those instead of className. Example: expect(badge).toHaveAttribute('data-variant', 'destructive') after adding data-variant={variant} to the component.

issues.description: Class-based assertion used to verify dragging state: expect(row.className).toContain('shadow'). CSS classes are implementation details that can change with styling refactors.
issues.rule: E2E Selectors - Never use Tailwind classes to select or assert state
issues.fix: Add a data-dragging={isDragging} attribute to the row element and assert on that: expect(row).toHaveAttribute('data-dragging', 'true').

issues.description: The tid() utility is used in many source components (mocked as returning data-testid in test files) but no test file calls getByTestId via tid() lookup in the test assertions themselves — tid() is only mocked, not integrated as a centralized test-id registry. This is consistent but means test IDs are maintained as string literals in both components and tests without a shared constant.
issues.rule: Testing - Test IDs with tid() for stable selectors
issues.fix: This is an acceptable pattern (mocking tid() in tests) but consider whether a shared tid constants file could prevent typos between component and test. Lower priority given current consistency.

issues.description: The useSupabase hook (apps/admin/src/shared/application/hooks/useSupabase.ts) has no corresponding test file. This is a shared infrastructure hook used across the admin app.
issues.fix: Add a unit test for useSupabase covering initialization and client availability.

issues.description: useClearCartCookie is a trivial one-liner (returns clearCartCookie directly) and has no test. Given its simplicity, this is acceptable under KISS — the underlying clearCartCookie is tested via cartCookie.test.ts.
issues.rule: KISS Guardrail - Don't require tests for trivial presentational components
issues.fix: No action required. The underlying cartCookie infrastructure is tested.

issues.description: useSupabaseAuth hooks exist in auth, admin, payments, store, studio, and playground apps with no test files. These are auth-critical hooks. However they may be thin wrappers around the Supabase SDK, so coverage depends on complexity.
issues.fix: Review each useSupabaseAuth hook to determine if it contains custom logic (error handling, session refresh, etc.). If so, add unit tests. If it is a pure delegation to the SDK, document the intentional lack of tests.

patterns.description: Excellent overall test coverage: 100+ test files across all apps (store, admin, payments, studio, auth, landing), covering hooks, components, pages, domain constants, infrastructure queries, and API routes.

Checkout stock integrity is well-tested: apps/payments/src/app/api/checkout/payment-methods/route.test.ts covers both the happy path (cart within stock) and the suppression path (cart exceeds stock returns empty methods array). This satisfies the checkout-stock-integrity rule.
Async handling is properly done across all hook tests: renderHook + act pattern used correctly, waitFor used in component tests where needed, findBy* used for async elements.
Test files consistently import all Vitest functions explicitly (describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach) satisfying the build-checks rule.

getByTestId is heavily used (630 occurrences across 105 files), and tid() is consistently mocked to return { 'data-testid': id } in test files. This is the correct pattern and aligns with the e2e-selectors rule.
Class-based assertions exist in 2 test files (RoleBadge.test.tsx and ProductTableRow.test.tsx). These are isolated violations but should be corrected.
